frameworks.name: spontaneous activity-driven cortical wiring
frameworks.one_line: Structured pre-eye-opening activity instructs feature-specific cortical circuitry

frameworks.name: feedforward Hebbian co-activation
frameworks.one_line: Co-activated iso-tuning neurons strengthen long-range horizontal links

frameworks.name: retinal wave propagation model
frameworks.one_line: Anatomically constrained retinal mosaic generates spatiotemporally patterned input

frameworks.name: orientation map and salt-and-pepper organization
frameworks.one_line: Cortical topographies compared between higher mammals and rodents

core_question: Can spontaneous retinal waves initialize feature-specific long-range horizontal connections in V1 before eye-opening?
key_assumptions: Retinal-wave-driven activity drives feedforward cortical co-activation
Iso-orientation co-activation suffices to wire long-range horizontal connections
Pre-eye-opening retinal mosaics generate spatiotemporally structured activity
manipulations: Structured retinal wave input vs equivalent random activity
Higher-mammal orientation-map cortex vs rodent salt-and-pepper cortex
key_findings: Propagating retinal waves coactivate similar-tuning neurons and wire long-range horizontal connections
Equivalent random retinal activity fails to induce such organized connectivity
Emerged long-range horizontal connections produce patterned cortical activity matching the orientation map
Model also reproduces feature-specific microcircuits in rodent salt-and-pepper organizations
interpretations: Long-range horizontal connections in V1 may originate from spatiotemporally structured retinal-wave input
Early peripheral spontaneous activity contributes significantly to cortical functional circuit development
